title: NCSC and National Medical Commission
focus: Covers the National Commission for Scheduled Castes and the structure of the National Medical Commission.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 026]
2.7.3. NATIONAL COMMISSION FOR SCHEDULED CASTES (NCSC)
NCSC submitted its Annual Report for 2023-24 to the President of India.
About National Commission for Scheduled Castes (NCSC)
- Constitutional body under Article 338 of the Constitution.
- Composition: Chairperson, a Vice-Chairperson, and 3 other members, appointed by the President.
- Power: It has the power of a civil court in certain matters.
- Tenure: Hold office for a term of 3 years and shall not be eligible for appointment for more than 2 terms.
- Key Functions:
 - Investigate and monitor all matters relating to the safeguards provided for the SC.
 - Inquire into specific complaints with respect to the deprivation of rights and safeguards of the Scheduled Castes, etc.

2.7.4. NATIONAL MEDICAL COMMISSION (NMC)
National Medical Commission (NMC) recently completed five years.
About National Medical Commission
- Establishment: Statutory body constituted under National Medical Commission Act, 2019; came into force in 2020.
 - Replaced Medical Council of India (MCI), and the Board of Governors was dissolved.
- Commission consists of the following persons to be appointed by the Central Government, namely:
 - a chairperson
 - ten ex officio Members
 - twenty-two part-time Members
- Four Autonomous boards under NMC:
 - Under- Graduate Medical Education Board
 - Post- Graduate Medical Education Board
 - Medical Assessment & Rating Board
 - Ethics and Medical Registration Board.

Functions of NMC:
- Policy & Standards: Lay down policies to maintain high standards in medical education and regulate institutions, research, and professionals.
- Healthcare Planning: Maps healthcare needs and resources effectively.
- Coordination: Ensures smooth functioning of boards and councils.
- Oversight: Monitors compliance and handles appeals efficiently.
- Ethics: Enforces professional conduct standards strictly.
- Fee Regulation: Controls fees for 50% seats in private/deemed colleges.
- Licensing: Grants limited licences to Community Health Providers.
